pullRequest.title: Update outdated dependencies
Update dependencies
Fix file name error while creating call graphs
Fix ValueError when APK score is negative
pullRequest.body:
Close #760 

When an APK receives a score higher than the total rule score or below zero, [`Weight.calculate()`](https://github.com/quark-engine/quark-engine/blob/d4493b3b2e686298a8ee4bda6841f1327ed671ce/quark/utils/weight.py#L21) raises a ValueError, which causes issue #760.  This PR enhances `Weight.calculate()` to handle APK scores in these two cases:
- If an APK receives scores higher than the total rule score, `Weight.calculate()` now categorizes it as **high-risk**.
- If an APK receives scores below zero, `Weight.calculate()` now categorizes it as **low-risk**.

pullRequest.title: Update version information to v25.8.1
Adjust CI trigger for GitHub release and PyPI release
Update GitHub release process in the docs
pullRequest.body:
### Release process


``Version: v2.0``

Quark releases a new version every month. To streamline this process, we run a GitHub Actions workflow augmented by LLM, which handles the following tasks automatically:

1. Open an issue listing all changes merged since the last release.
2. Create a PR to update the version number and compose the changelog.
3. Generate a release draft.

The workflow is scheduled to start on the Monday preceding the first Wednesday of each month. On that day, we follow the process below to complete the release.

![](https://i.postimg.cc/gYVXBDMp/github-release-drawio-14.png)


>  [!Note]
> The release process starts on the Monday preceding the first Wednesday of each month. For example, the process for the **v25.10.1** release starts on **Monday, September 29, 2025**, since the following **Wednesday, October 1, 2025**, is the first Wednesday of October.



There are 6 steps in the process:

**Step 1. (Optional) Manually initiate the workflow for the GitHub release.**

>  [!Note]
> Normally, this step is unnecessary because the workflow will start automatically. If it fails to start, you can initiate it manually using the method below.

Click the ``Actions`` tab on Quark's GitHub page.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/MHW9J1nc/tpBB18r.png)

Click the ``Generate GitHub Release Issue/PR`` workflow in the left sidebar.

![](https://i.postimg.cc/7YDJ7Hpn/Screenshot-2025-08-17-11-55-37.png)


Click the gray `Run workflow` button and then the green `Run workflow` button. The workflow will create an issue and a PR approximately 5 minutes later. 
![](https://i.postimg.cc/63hhcTMB/Screenshot-2025-08-11-16-07-17.png)


The issue lists all PRs merged since the last release.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/MGcGcstT/issue.jpg)


And the PR updates the version number and changelog.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/MKc3FVsB/pr.jpg)


**Step 2. Ensure the issue lists all changes since the last release.**

If the auto-generated issue omits any changes since the last release, edit the issue manually to include them. To edit the issue, click the ``...`` menu on the right and select ``Edit``.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/hPM6kKgF/Screenshot-2025-08-12-07-35-15.png)

**Step 3. Test whether the new changes work correctly and do not break any features or downstream projects.**

First, verify that all change listed in the issue works as intended.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/MGcGcstT/issue.jpg)

Next, verify that all CI checks for the PR have passed.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/2CqZ5xDv/cicheck.jpg) 

Then, verify that Quark runs correctly in downstream projects such as Jadx and APKLab, and attach the result screenshots to the issue.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/G2LdFqxG/jadx.jpg)



**Step 4. Fix the problems caused by the changes.**

If the changes do not work correctly or break any features or downstream projects, fix the problem.

**Step 5. Ensure the PR is correct and merge it.**



The PR should update the version number in:
- `debian/control`
- `docs/source/conf.py`
- `quark/__init__.py`

The PR should update the changelog in:
- `debian/changelog`



If the auto-generated PR does not correctly update the version number and changelog, edit them manually by pushing your changes to the branch ``update_version_info_{VERSION_NUMBER}``. For example, if you want to edit the PR for the **v25.8.1** release, push your changes to the branch ``update_version_info_v25.8.1``.

![](https://i.postimg.cc/zJtDRMGd/pr-e.jpg)


To merge the PR, first click the dropdown button 🔽 and select ``Squash and merge``.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/4x2SLwGp/merge-pr-1.jpg)

Next, click the ``Squash and merge`` button.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/T3Bc5sGN/merge-pr-2.jpg)

Verify the commit message. Then, click the ``Confirm squash and merge`` button to complete the merge.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/hPyscjYB/merge-pr-3.jpg)


**Step 6. Ensure the release draft is accurate and publish it.**

Click the `Releases` link on Quark’s GitHub page, and you can see the auto-generated release draft.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/SN6XftWt/release-01.jpg)

Click the pencil button.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/X7GmnSx4/Screenshot-2025-08-12-07-47-19-1.png)

If the auto-generated release draft cannot accurately describe the changes, edit it manually to provide a precise description. You can edit the release draft in the `Write` tab.
![](https://i.postimg.cc/pTrkkz6R/Screenshot-2025-08-12-07-48-19.png)

To publish the release, click the ``Publish release`` button.
[![Screenshot-2025-08-12-07-48-56.png](https://i.postimg.cc/c1gGLn4p/Screenshot-2025-08-12-07-48-56.png)](https://postimg.cc/c6SbD63h)

pullRequest.body: #### Description This PR fixes #827 with the following changes: 1. Add support for invoke-direct/range. 2. Fix PyEval treating multiple registers referencing the same object as different objects. 3. Fix PyEval not tracking internal state changes of an object. 4. Reduce memory usage by removing duplicated parameters stored in PyEval. **Tests** - [x] All unit tests passed - [x] Verify that the relevant issue is fixed.

Update version information to v26.1.1
Fix Quark crashes due to invalid values in APK ZIP header and AndroidManifest header
pullRequest.body:
Closes #858.

This PR introduces an `ApkPatcher` to check and fix 2 invalid values in APKs that cause Quark crashes.

1.  **Invalid ZIP Compression Method**  
    It scans ZIP headers and patches any invalid compression method values to 0. Since 0 indicates the data is uncompressed, it also updates the compressed size to match the uncompressed size. This prevents a `NotImplementedError`.

2.  **Invalid AndroidManifest Header**  
    It looks for `AndroidManifest.xml` in the APK and corrects its first byte to `0x03`. After the correction, it updates the CRC-32 checksum in the ZIP file. This ensures successful AXML parsing.

The patcher is integrated as a pre-processing step before Quark analysis.

### Tests

- [x] Add unit tests for the `ApkPatcher`.
- [x] Ensure all unit tests pass.

Fix #867: Separate filled-new-array parsing from invoke-kind
pullRequest.body:
Fix #867.

This PR addresses the issue by:
- Handling filled-new-array-kind instructions directly rather than delegating to `_invoke` function.
- Fix `_invoke` incorrectly parsing empty strings as parameter types for methods without parameters

### Tests

- [x] Add unit tests for FILLED_NEW_ARRAY_KIND.
- [x] Ensure all unit tests pass.

pullRequest.body:
Fix #876.

This PR addresses the issue by:
- updating the codex CLI to the latest version (0.110.0)
- refactoring workflow commands to use the new codex CLI options.

This fix has been verified on a personal fork. Please check [here](https://github.com/haeter525/quark-engine/actions/runs/22722707216) for details.

pullRequest.title: Fix #889: bears_object() now recognizes array types as objects
pullRequest.body:
Closes #889

**Key Changes:**
- [registerobject.py](https://github.com/ev-flow/quark-engine/blob/swe-agent-fix-%23889-53135393/quark/core/struct/registerobject.py): extend `bears_object()` to return `True` for array types starting with `[`
- [test_registerobject.py](https://github.com/ev-flow/quark-engine/blob/swe-agent-fix-%23889-53135393/tests/core/struct/test_registerobject.py): add test cases for `[B`, `[Ljava/lang/String;`, and `[[B` types
- [smoke_test.yml](https://github.com/ev-flow/quark-engine/blob/swe-agent-fix-%23889-53135393/.github/workflows/smoke_test.yml): restore expected Ahmyth detection count to 39

**Tests:**
- [x] `pytest tests/core/struct/test_registerobject.py` — all `test_bears_object` cases pass
- [x] Smoke test against `Ahmyth.apk` reports 39 behaviors at 100% confidence for Rule 00015
